title: Move group-header status decor from .status-badge onto .workspace-chevron
action: In renderWorkspaceSection (switcher.ts:269-293), delete the renderGroupBadge call and the renderGroupBadge function itself (per D1) -- no .status-badge stays in the workspace-header. Give .workspace-chevron the status decor the badge used to carry (per D2): icon color = --status-{working|blocked|done|idle|unknown} driven by group.workspace_status, plus a circular wash background using the matching --status-*-wash token, plus the SAME @keyframes pulse (working) / blink (blocked) animation .status-badge/.status-dot already define (styles.css:587-657). Put the wash background and the pulse/blink animation on a wrapper element or ::before around the <svg>, never on the svg's own transform -- the svg keeps its existing rotate(90deg)<->rotate(0deg) mechanic untouched (per D3); the icon's static color property is safe to set directly on the svg since it does not touch transform. Preserve hsw-D7 exactly: a group with zero agent rows (workspace_status === null) gets a plain chevron, no color/wash/animation at all -- same short-circuit renderGroupBadge used, just applied to whether decor classes are added instead of whether a badge renders. A group whose real workspace_status is "unknown" gets the same color+wash treatment as any other status, no animation (per D5) -- do not fold it into the null/no-agents case. Add accessible text carrying STATUS_LABEL[status] (switcher.ts:35-41) for screen readers now that the visible text pill is gone (per D4): add a new visually-hidden utility (e.g. a .sr-only rule -- clip to 1px, not display:none) and a span using it inside .workspace-header-label near the chevron, so the button's existing accessible name (the group label) is supplemented, not replaced. Update web/test/switcher.test.ts: the 'hides the header badge on a shell-only group while an agent group keeps its badge' test (currently asserting .status-badge presence/absence at lines ~320-333) must assert the new chevron-decor contract instead (agent group's chevron carries a status class, shell-only group's chevron stays plain); the 'no status badge' assertion at line ~291 stays valid (.status-badge is still gone from shell rows) but add an equivalent chevron-plain assertion for consistency. Add a new test case for an "unknown" workspace_status group asserting the chevron gets color+wash decor. Do not touch renderAgentCard's individual per-row status badge (switcher.ts ~200-225) -- that badge is out of scope and must keep working exactly as today.

truths: No .workspace-header in the rendered DOM ever contains a .status-badge element, for any group (agent or shell-only)

An agent group's .workspace-chevron carries its status color, wash background, and (for working/blocked) the reused pulse/blink animation, without breaking the existing aria-expanded-driven rotate transform
A shell-only (zero-agent) group's .workspace-chevron stays plain -- no color, wash, or animation classes -- exactly as hsw-D7 required for the old badge
A group with workspace_status "unknown" gets the same color+wash chevron treatment as any other real status, with no animation
The workspace-header button exposes the group's status label as accessible text for screen readers in addition to the visible group name, whenever workspace_status is non-null

artifacts.substantive: renderGroupBadge and its call site removed; renderWorkspaceSection renders chevron status classes + accessible status text; no leftover dead code or unused STATUS_LABEL-adjacent imports
new chevron status-decor rules (per-status color, circular wash, reused pulse/blink keyframes on a non-rotating wrapper) plus a visually-hidden utility rule for the accessible status text
badge-presence assertions replaced with chevron-decor assertions; new test case covers the unknown-status chevron treatment

key_links: renderWorkspaceSection's chevron markup reads group.workspace_status through the same STATUS_LABEL lookup renderGroupBadge used
prohibitions: Do not change .status-badge/.status-dot definitions or their use on individual agent rows (switcher.ts ~200-225) -- only the group-header usage is removed
Do not change the rotate mechanic's selectors or values ([aria-expanded="false"] .workspace-chevron rotate(0deg), default rotate(90deg))
Do not apply the pulse animation's transform:scale directly to .workspace-chevron's own transform property

verify: cd web && npm run test -- --run switcher && npm run typecheck

trace.outcome: Removed .status-badge from workspace group headers; .workspace-chevron now carries status color, wash background, and reused pulse/blink decor via a non-rotating .workspace-chevron-wrap wrapper, with a new .sr-only accessible status span; unknown status gets color+wash, no animation; zero-agent groups stay plain per D7.
trace.verify_output: vitest: Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 21 passed (21); tsc --noEmit: no output (clean)
trace.verify_command: cd web && npm run test -- --run switcher && npm run typecheck
